rulings.Case Title: ANGIE CHRISTENSEN VS. WILL LIGHTBOURNE, DIRECTOR, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 24, 2014, Line 2, PETITIONER ANGIE CHRISTENSEN'S Motion For Writ Of Mandate And Declaratory Relief. The court grants the petition for administrative mandamus and declaratory relief. First, California Welfare and Institutions Code sec. 11451.5 and AB 1542 do not appear to be an exhaustive list of what matters are exempt in calculating income. Second, respondent's position is contrary to its own definition of income. Respondent's regulation (sec. 44-101) provides that "income" is a cash benefit that is "currently available to the individual or is received by him." The income "must, in fact, be currently available to needy members of the family in meeting their needs¿" Here, the child support that is transferred to children that live outside the home is not available to needy members of the family. Welfare and Institutions Code sec. 11450.12(b) similarly refers to "reasonably anticipated income." Third, respondent's interpretation of the governing scheme is contrary to Welfare and Institutions code sec. 11005.5. Double counting should not occur among recipient groups. In Rogers v. Dietrich (1976) 58 Cal.App.3d 90, 101, the court aptly explains: "To treat one person's aid as a reason to deny eligibility or to reduce assistance to which another is entitled amounts to defiance of the legislative proscription." The court denies petitioner's request for traditional mandamus. Petitioner's remedy is to pursue administrative mandamus given the underlying hearing. In addition, the statutory landscape regarding the exemption was unclear and petitioner does not cite a clear ministerial statutory duty. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PROGRESSIVE DIRECT INSURANCE COMPANY VS. MARK S. DAVIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Reopen Discovery And $1,488.00 In Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Arthur Meirson
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 24, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT MARK DAVIS, BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC. Motion To Reopen Discovery And $1,488.00 In Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Arthur Meirson. Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Discovery is reopened until 30 days before trial. Motion for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARGARITO MEZA et al VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 24, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS MARGARITO MEZA, EDELMIRA MEZA'S Motion For An Order Granting Leave To File Amendment To Complaint Substituting Party Defendant For Doe One. Granted. Plaintiffs may file their proposed amendment to the complaint substituting Pacific Bell Telephone Company for Doe 1. "The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified." (Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428.) rulings.Case Title: MABEL LIN VS. R.J. MCGLENNON COMPANY, INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Defendant Michael Mcglennon
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 24, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANTS R.J. MCGLENNON COMPANY, INC., MICHAEL MCGLENNON'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Defendant Michael Mcglennon. Denied. 29 U.S.C. sec. 2611(4)(A)(i) provides that employees can be held individually liable. (See Mitchell v. Chapman (6th Cir. 2003) 343 F.3d 811, 827.) The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") sufficiently alleges that Defendant Michael McGlennon was Plaintiff's supervisor and exercised control over her ability to take leave. (FAC, par. 23.) rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING VS. GLENDONBROOK WINES PTY LTD, A PRESUMED AUSTRALIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 24, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT AMERICAN GUARANTEE AND LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The motion is granted. Defendant establishes that this is a SLAPP suit. Plaintiff's claims are based on defendant's retention of counsel (Lewis Brisbois) and defendant's litigation conduct in the malpractice action (case #507519-Gidding v. Salama). "All communicative acts performed by attorneys as part of their representation of a client in a judicial proceeding or other petitioning context are per se protected as petitioning activity by the anti-SLAPP statute." Cabral v. Martins (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 471, 480. Therefore, the burden has shifted to plaintiff to present evidence showing a probability of prevailing on the merits. Plaintiff has failed to maintain his burden. First, plaintiff fails to present evidence to support the claims pleaded in the amended complaint. Plaintiff's opposition focuses on tax fraud claims, but the amended complaint does not allege such claims. Second, Civil Code 47 [litigation privilege] bars plaintiff's claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: IGOR KRAYCHIK VS. EXIGEN (USA), INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, November 24, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANTS EXIGEN HOLDINGS, INC., EXIGEN SERVICES, INC., EXIGEN, LTD., EXIGEN PROPERTIES, INC., EXIFAST'S Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action. Petition granted as to Defendants Exigen Holdings, Inc., Exigen Properties, Inc., and Exifast. Petition is denied as to Defendants Exigen Services, Inc. and Exigen, Ltd. in light of the ruling on Plaintiff's Motion to Strike. A nonsignatory defendant may invoke an arbitration clause to compel a signatory plaintiff to arbitrate its claims when the causes of action against the nonsignatory are "intimately founded in and intertwined" with the underlying contract obligations. (Boucher v. Alliance Title Company, Inc. (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 262, 271.) By relying on contract terms in its claims against Defendants, Plaintiff is equitably estopped from repudiating the arbitration clause contained in that agreement. (Id. at 272.) Furthermore, Plaintiff has produced insufficient evidence to establish that the arbitration agreement was superseded and nullified, nor is the agreement unconscionable. The evidence presented establishes that the employment contract was negotiable as to job title, bonus, salary, moving allowance, and start date ( Kraychik Decl., Ex. 1), and Plaintiff failed to present evidence that any other provision in the Offer Letter was not similarly negotiable. Furthermore, a failure to include or provide the arbitration rules referenced in the agreement alone is insufficient to support a finding of procedural unconscionability where the rules affect only Plaintiff's procedural rights. ((2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1472.) Nor is the arbitration agreement substantively unconscionable. Plaintiff's argument as to lack of mutuality lacks merit because the agreement specifically limits its application to the employment relationship between Plaintiff and the signatory Defendant only, and the Confidentiality and Invention Assignment Agreement alone does not compel a finding that the agreement lacks mutuality. Further, the qualifying language in the attorneys' fees provision keeps it from running afoul of Armendariz's minimum requirement that "the arbitration agreement ... cannot generally require the employee to bear any type of expense that the employee would not be required to bear if he or she were free to bring the action in court." (Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, 110-11.) Additionally, the mere possibility of the "repeat player effect"-without any evidence-is not enough to render an arbitration agreement unconscionable, and the agreement is not subject to unilateral modification or revocation. Defendants' request to stay the action pending completion of the arbitration proceedings is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
